title: quercus lanuginosa var. lacinifolia
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. lacinifolia, also known as the hairy-leafed or laciniate-leafed evergreen oak, is a variety of evergreen oak tree native to China, characterized by its unique, laciniate (sliced or cut) leaves. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Quercus lanuginosa var. lacinifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable addition to traditional Chinese medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being dried and used in capsule or powder form for convenient consumption.
